name: Norway

group.value: Group I — opponents France, Senegal, Iraq

manager.value: Ståle Solbakken — high-pressing, possession-based with fast counterattacking transitions

qualification.value: QUALIFIED — won their UEFA qualifying group with a perfect 8 wins from 8, scoring 37 goals and conceding 5, including home-and-away wins over Italy. First World Cup since 1998 (a 28-year absence).

recent_form.value: Unbeaten, dominant qualifying campaign (8/8 wins, 37 goals); Haaland scored 16 in qualifying — roughly double any other European player

wc_role.value: Returning dark horse — qualified for their first World Cup since 1998 (ended a 28-year absence); Haaland's first World Cup

key_players.value: Erling Haaland (Manchester City; 16 qualifying goals; ~55 goals in ~49 caps for Norway), Martin Ødegaard (Arsenal captain, creative hub), Alexander Sørloth, Antonio Nusa, Oscar Bobb

squad_news.value: Solbakken named his 26-man squad in May 2026, headlined by Haaland and Ødegaard. Ødegaard's season was disrupted by multiple injuries but he is in the squad.
